name: reconnect_switch_add_second_connector
notes: RE-connect / add-a-SECOND-connector beat, distinct from the FIRST-connect decline/pivot in i_sheets_shopify_decline_pivot_to_sheets.json (which is a fresh tenant declining the Shopify offer and pivoting to Sheets). Here the tenant already has a live data source: --seed-lapsed-customers writes a real tenant_connector_status row (connector_id='google_sheet', enabled=TRUE, last_status='ok') — the same 'already-connected source' substrate reconnect_broken_sync_honesty.json and recurring_pull_cadence_change.json use. No --flow, so the completed journey has no __flow__ sentinel and an owner-initiated request reaches the integration agent directly (verify_connector reads the seeded google_sheet status; there is no shopify state, so start_oauth for Shopify is a genuine NEW connect). The owner asks to ALSO add Shopify. Exercises the agent starting a real new Shopify connect (offer / ask-for-domain / mint) WITHOUT (a) fabricating that Shopify is already connected, or (b) claiming the existing Google Sheet was removed/replaced. Setup: `setup --onboarded --seed-lapsed-customers 6` then `script <tenant> reconnect_switch_to_supported_connector.json`.

steps.message: you've already got my Google Sheet hooked up — now I also want to connect my Shopify store, add that too
assert_not_contains: your shopify store is now connected
steps.note: Add a SECOND connector while one is already live. Expect the agent to start a genuine new Shopify connect (ask for the store address / offer the link) that names Shopify — never a fabricated claim that Shopify is already connected.

steps.message: sure, my Shopify address is sundaram-sweets.myshopify.com
assert_not_contains: your google sheet is now disconnected
steps.note: Owner supplies the store address. Expect the connect to proceed (a real authorize link-out / tap-this-link instructions) with no fabricated 'already connected' status and no claim the existing Google Sheet was dropped or replaced by adding Shopify.
